LanguageData.Kopp_CISE_ConfirmLoadConfig: Really load config {0}?\n\nIf the config you are using right now is not saved it will be lost if you proceed.
LanguageData.Kopp_CISE_DefaultConfigRecreated: The default config has been recreated! The old default config held {0} thingDefs while the new default config holds {1} thingDefs.
LanguageData.Kopp_CISE_UseVanillaPawnsTab: Set this to true to show the vanilla pawns tab instead of the mods one.\nIt might be useful/necessary if you use mods that alter the vanilla pawns tab.\nThis setting gets applied to all configs.
LanguageData.Kopp_CISE_ContentPawnsTab:
The content of the pawns tab is hard coded
and can not be changed.



The tab can be renamed and its position can
be switched though.
LanguageData.Kopp_CISE_ConfigSaved: The config is saved as {0}. Enter a valid name to rename it.
LanguageData.Kopp_CISE_ConfigGainsNameFail: The name {0} is either invalid or already in use. Choose another one.
LanguageData.Kopp_CISE_Helper_MainTabs:
The mod has 2 different windows: 'Tab settings' and 'Subcategory settings'. 
Vanilla has three tabs: Pawns, Travel Supplies and Items. 
In Vanilla only the Pawns tab has subcategories: Colonists, Prisoners, Slaves, Animals, ... 
In the row below you can see all the tabs that your config has and switch between them and then do changes.
LanguageData.Kopp_CISE_Helper_FilterUI:
The filter UI: Allow items that should be shown in the corresponding tab. You can then attach these items to subcategories in the subcategory settings if you like. 
Items can appear in more than one tab. 
Things on the map that are in no tab will appear in a new tab called 'Unassigned' automatically. This tab is not visible in the tab settings as it can not be changed. 
However: You can easily make a new tab with all the things that you have not assigned to a tab yet by using the button 'New tab from saved config' on the right side of the gui, then select 'tmpConfig', then 'Unassigned'. Note that if you did not assign items of a certain quality (e.g. legendary weapons) or with a certain hitpoints percentage to a tab, the copied unassigned tab will only have the ones allowed, that are on the map right now. And every quality level/hitpoint percentage is allowed.
LanguageData.Kopp_CISE_Helper_CategoryTabs: Add a new tab, either a blank one or a (independent) copy of a existing one. The copy will have all subcategories of the parent included and all allowed items of the parent selected. \nThere is a maximum of 15 tabs and a maximum of 15 subcategories per tab.
LanguageData.Kopp_CISE_Helper_Configs:
A config contains all the infos needed to 'draw' the tabs and their content in the caravan/transporter gui. E.g. the number of tabs, the names, the subcategories and the selected items. 
Imagine having a sheet of paper in front of you with that infos. This sheet of paper will ALWAYS be used to draw the tabs. All the changes you do will ALWAYS be done to that sheet, too. It is called 'tmpConfig'. Next to the sheet is a stack of papers, which represent the saved configs. 
If you load a config, the corresponding paper of the stack gets copied and is replacing the sheet in front of you. If you save a config the sheet in front of you gets copied and the copy gets put on the stack of papers. 
That means if you save a config and make changes to it afterwards these changes apply to the sheet in front of you but not to the saved config. 
As a result it is not possible to overwrite a config. You have to delete the old one and save the new one. Configs are global, not per savegame. Your changes are either saved immediately or when closing the tab settings menu. The config you see when using this mod for the first time is a copy of 'defaultConfig'. If you only work with one config you dont have to worry about saving/loading or the config system at all. Just make changes and be happy. 
The 'recreate default config' button may be useful if you add/remove mods while using this mod so the default config gets updated with new items.
LanguageData.Kopp_CISE_Helper_Close: To close the helper overlay either press the button below, ESC, the button you used to open the overlay or use the close button in the top right corner. Sorry for the bad implementation...
LanguageData.Kopp_CISE_NeedsRestart: The following settings need a restart to be applied!
LanguageData.Kopp_CISE_ShowLoadingListTooltip: If set to true, you can see a list of all the items/pawns already selected when forming a caravan (or splitting, or loading transporters).
LanguageData.Kopp_CISE_ShowSelectEveryhing: Show the button 'Select everything' in the caravan forming dialog, regardless of whether the dev mode is acive:
LanguageData.Kopp_CISE_ReformCaravanDropdown: Show a dropdown menu when clicking 'Reform caravan' to define if items and pawns should be preselected:
LanguageData.Kopp_CISE_ReformSelectNone: Do not preselect any items or pawns
LanguageData.Kopp_CISE_UnselectedStuff: The following things could not be selected:
LanguageData.Kopp_CISE_PresetContains: The preset contains the following things:
LanguageData.Kopp_CISE_LoadOnTop: Load on top of the current selection
LanguageData.Kopp_CISE_OverwriteConfirmation: Are you sure you want to overwrite this preset with the current selection?
LanguageData.Kopp_CISE_DeleteConfirmation: Are you sure you want to delete this preset?
LanguageData.Kopp_CISE_SavePreset: Save the current selection as a new preset
LanguageData.Kopp_CISE_UnselectedStuffAgain: Show again what could not be selected
LanguageData.Kopp_CISE_HitpointRangeTooltip:
If set to a different value than 100%, the mod takes hitpoints of items (that have a quality or are buildings) into account when loading a preset.

Example: You save a preset with a revolver that has 60% hitpoints left and you set this value to 20%.
When loading the preset there needs to be a revolver available that has between 40% and 80% hitpoints left (and has the same quality as the saved one), otherwise no revolver gets selected.
